prompt: White to move. Mate in 1!
explanation: The back rank is undefended and the king is trapped by its own pawns. Ra8 is checkmate.

prompt: White to move. Mate in 1!
explanation: The classic Scholar's Mate: the queen captures on f7, protected by the bishop on c4. The king cannot escape or capture.

prompt: White to move. Mate in 1!
explanation: A smothered mate! The black king is completely surrounded by its own pieces, so the knight check ends the game.

prompt: White to move. Mate in 1!
explanation: The queen covers the whole back rank while your king guards all the escape squares in front.

prompt: White to move. Mate in 1!
explanation: The Arabian mate: the knight protects the rook and covers g8, so the rook check on the h-file is final.

prompt: White to move. Mate in 1!
explanation: Boden's mate: two bishops slice across the king on crossing diagonals while its own pieces block the exits.

prompt: White to move. Mate in 1!
explanation: Lolli's mate: the pawn on f6 protects the queen as it lands on g7, right next to the trapped king.

prompt: White to move. Mate in 1!
explanation: The hook mate: the rook checks beside the king, protected by the knight, which is protected by the pawn. Every escape square is covered.

prompt: White to move. Mate in 1!
explanation: Capture and checkmate at the same time! The rook was the only guard of the back rank.

prompt: White to move. Mate in 2!
explanation: The rook check forces black to block on e8 — then you capture the blocker and it is mate on the back rank.

prompt: White to move. Mate in 2!
explanation: The famous smothered mate: sacrifice the queen on g8 to force the rook to trap its own king, then the knight delivers mate on f7.

prompt: White to move. Mate in 2!
explanation: Anastasia's mate: the knight check seals g8, and the rook swings to the open h-file for checkmate.

prompt: White to move. Mate in 2!
explanation: The ladder (lawnmower) mate: one rook cuts off the 7th rank, the other delivers mate on the 8th.

prompt: White to move. Mate in 2! (From Morphy's Opera Game)
explanation: Paul Morphy's legendary finish: the queen sacrifice deflects the knight, and the rook mates on d8, protected by the bishop on g5.

prompt: White to move. Win material with a fork!
explanation: A royal fork! The knight checks the king and attacks the queen at the same time. After the king moves, take the queen.

prompt: White to move. Win a piece with a pawn fork!
explanation: The little pawn attacks two big pieces at once. Black can only save one of them.

prompt: White to move. Fork the king and rook!
explanation: The queen checks the king and eyes the loose rook on a8 along the same diagonal. Check first, then collect.

prompt: White to move. The classic knight fork!
explanation: Nc7+ is one of the most famous forks in chess — king and rook attacked at once from a square the king cannot reach.

prompt: White to move. One knight, two targets!
explanation: The knight hop attacks the queen and the rook simultaneously. Whatever black saves, you capture the other — with check!

prompt: White to move. Use the pin to win a piece!
explanation: The knight is pinned to the king by your bishop, so it cannot run. Attack it with the pawn and it is lost.

prompt: White to move. Punish the pinned knight!
explanation: The knight is pinned to the queen. When you attack it with the pawn, moving it loses the queen — and staying loses the knight.

prompt: White to move. Pin and win the queen!
explanation: The bishop pins the queen to the king. The queen's only option is to capture — but the a4-pawn defends the bishop!

prompt: White to move. Skewer the king and queen!
explanation: The rook check forces the king to step aside, revealing the queen behind it on the same rank.

prompt: White to move. A long-range skewer!
explanation: The bishop checks along the long diagonal. When the king moves, the rook behind it on a8 falls.

prompt: White to move. Unleash a discovered check!
explanation: Moving the knight opens the rook's line to the king AND attacks the queen. Black must deal with the check, so the queen is lost.

prompt: White to move. Deflect the defender!
explanation: The rook on b8 is the only piece stopping your pawn. Attack it — if it captures, your pawn promotes with check!

prompt: White to move. A free piece is waiting!
explanation: Always scan for undefended pieces. The knight on d5 has no defender — take it!

prompt: White to move. Spot the free piece!
explanation: The bishop on c4 is completely undefended. In your games, check every capture before you move.

prompt: White to move. The biggest prize of all!
explanation: The black queen is hanging on the open a-file. Capturing the queen usually wins the game.

prompt: White to move. Win material on the open file!
explanation: Two rooks face off on the d-file, but the black rook has no defender — and capturing it is even checkmate on the back rank!

prompt: White to move. Attack two pieces at once!
explanation: From a4 the queen attacks the rook on e8 and the bishop on h4 at the same time. Black cannot save both.
